title: Search API Endpoint

story.i_want_to: search the knowledge wiki through a REST endpoint with multiple search modes
story.so_that: I can find relevant knowledge articles efficiently using the most appropriate search strategy

rationale: The context functions built in US-20.1 and US-20.4 need an HTTP interface for agents and developers to use. A unified search endpoint with a `mode` parameter lets callers choose keyword search (fast, exact), semantic search (conceptual matching), or combined (best of both). Returning snippets instead of full bodies keeps responses small and fast, letting agents decide which articles to fetch in full. The on-the-fly query embedding for semantic/combined modes means callers only need to send a text query, not pre-compute embeddings.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/knowledge/search is accessible with role agent+. Returns 401/403 for unauthenticated or insufficient role.
Required query parameter: `q` (search query text). Returns 400 with error message if `q` is missing or empty.

Optional query parameters: `project_id` (UUID), `category` (string), `tags` (comma-separated string, split into list), `mode` (keyword|semantic|combined, default: combined), `limit` (integer, default 10, max 50), `offset` (integer, default 0).

When mode=keyword, calls `Knowledge.search_keyword/3`. When mode=semantic, generates query embedding on-the-fly and calls `Knowledge.search_semantic/3`. When mode=combined, calls `Knowledge.search_combined/3`.

Each result includes: `id`, `title`, `category`, `tags`, `relevance_score` (from the search function), and `snippet` (first 300 characters of body or ts_headline for keyword mode). Does NOT include full body content.

For semantic and combined modes, the query embedding is generated on-the-fly by calling `@embedding_client.generate_embedding/1` with the `q` parameter. If embedding generation fails in combined mode, falls back to keyword-only with `meta.fallback: true`.
For semantic mode only, if embedding generation fails, returns 503 Service Unavailable with an error message indicating the embedding service is down.
Response includes pagination meta: `total_count`, `limit`, `offset`. Response shape: `{"data": [...], "meta": {...}}`.
Invalid `mode` parameter returns 400 with error listing valid modes.
All results are scoped to the current tenant. The endpoint respects RLS.

test_cases.name: keyword search via API returns snippets without body
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Testing Patterns', body: 'Use ExUnit for unit tests. Property-based testing with StreamData...', status: :published})
expected_results: Response status is 200
Response data includes article with title 'Testing Patterns'
Article has relevance_score, snippet, id, title, category, tags
Article does NOT have full body content
Snippet is at most 300 characters

test_cases.name: combined search mode is the default
preconditions: MockEmbeddingClient returns valid embedding for query
steps: GET /api/v1/knowledge/search?q=patterns (no mode param) with agent API key
expected_results: Response status is 200
Combined search was executed (both keyword and semantic)
Results include final_score (combined relevance)

test_cases.name: missing q parameter returns 400
steps: GET /api/v1/knowledge/search (no q param) with agent API key
GET /api/v1/knowledge/search?q= (empty q) with agent API key
expected_results: Both return 400 Bad Request
Error message indicates q parameter is required

test_cases.name: semantic mode returns 503 when embedding service is down
expected_results: Response status is 503 Service Unavailable
Error message indicates embedding service is unavailable

test_cases.name: filters by project_id, category, and tags
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, category: 'guides', tags: ['elixir', 'phoenix'], title: 'Phoenix Guide', body: 'LiveView patterns...', status: :published})
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: nil, category: 'ops', tags: ['docker'], title: 'Docker Guide', body: 'Container patterns...', status: :published})
expected_results: project_id filter returns only the Phoenix Guide
category filter returns only the Phoenix Guide
tags filter returns only the Docker Guide

test_cases.name: invalid mode returns 400
expected_results: Response status is 400
Error message lists valid modes: keyword, semantic, combined

test_cases.name: pagination with limit and offset
preconditions: Create 15 published articles with 'pattern' in the body
expected_results: First call returns 5 results with meta.total_count=15
Second call returns 5 different results with meta.total_count=15
meta.limit=5 and meta.offset matches the request

test_cases.name: tenant isolation in search API
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, title: 'Secret Knowledge', body: 'Proprietary patterns...', status: :published})
expected_results: Returns empty results
Tenant A's articles are not searchable by Tenant B

technical_notes: Tags parsing: `String.split(tags_param, ",") |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1) |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))` to handle comma-separated input.
Mode validation: use a simple case statement or enum check — `mode in ["keyword", "semantic", "combined"]`. Default to "combined" when not specified.
Snippet generation: for keyword mode, ts_headline already provides highlighted snippets. For semantic/combined modes, truncate body to 300 characters: `String.slice(body, 0, 300)`.

On-the-fly embedding: `@embedding_client.generate_embedding(q)` — same compile-time DI pattern. For semantic-only mode, embedding failure is fatal (503). For combined mode, it triggers fallback to keyword-only.
Response view: create `LoopctlWeb.KnowledgeSearchJSON` module that renders the search results with the standardized shape.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.

The controller (or context function) resolves the embedding client via @embedding_client Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingClient) — same config key as the Oban worker in US-20.3.
